name: clauding-thought
description: AI code governance framework. Analyzes codebases and generates project-specific .claude/ governance directories with rules, patterns, security checks, and agent skills.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Bootstrap governance layer. Analyzes the codebase and generates manifest, rules, patterns, skills, and changelog.

commands.name: preflight
commands.description: Gathers context before coding. Classifies the task, loads relevant rules and patterns, finds sibling files, identifies risks.

commands.name: qc
commands.description: Quality control review. Evaluates uncommitted changes against manifest-driven security, architecture, and convention rules.

commands.name: evolve
commands.description: Re-analyzes the codebase and updates the governance layer. Detects drift, suggests rule changes, bumps version.

commands.name: task-doc
commands.description: Creates or updates a task document for the current work. Tracks decisions, files changed, QC verdicts.

commands.name: close-task
commands.description: Finalizes the active task document. Records commit reference, captures lessons learned, promotes learnings to memory.

commands.name: export
commands.description: Exports governance rules to other AI coding tools (Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf).

commands.name: report
commands.description: Governance health report. Analyzes task history, QC verdicts, and governance evolution.

commands.name: insights
commands.description: Cross-project intelligence. Analyzes anonymized findings to identify patterns and suggest rule/hook improvements.

commands.name: critique
commands.description: Adversarial code review that finds what QC misses. Challenges logic, design, resilience, and maintainability.
